stories.title: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
stories.simple_summary: On the music streaming service Deezer, more than half of the songs uploaded every day are now made by artificial intelligence instead of human musicians. That's over 90,000 AI songs per day.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use music streaming services, you may start hearing more computer-made music mixed in with human artists — though you probably won't notice the difference when listening.

stories.title: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
stories.simple_summary: A company called Halliday makes glasses that show a small screen, kind of like a tiny TV built into your eyeglasses. They've made a new version with a better screen that's easier to look at.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech enthusiasts — nothing you need to act on unless you're interested in new gadget trends.

stories.title: Opinion | The AI tax movement is built on a myth
stories.simple_summary: An opinion piece discusses the idea of taxing AI, saying some claims about why we need this tax may not be accurate.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for policy debate — nothing that affects your day-to-day life right now.

stories.title: Las Vegas hospitality braces for AI, but automation leads the way
stories.simple_summary: Hotels and casinos in Las Vegas are getting ready for AI technology, and many are replacing workers with robots and automation machines.
stories.why_it_matters: If you work in hospitality or live near Las Vegas, this shows that businesses are starting to use machines for jobs that people used to do.

stories.title: America needs to stop getting shocked by Chinese AI
stories.simple_summary: Companies in China have created AI systems that work as well as the best AI from American companies like OpenAI. This surprised American business leaders and politicians.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and government officials — it shows competition in AI is global, but it doesn't change how you use AI in your daily life.

stories.title: EXCLUSIVE: US, China to hold AI talks in September, sources say
stories.simple_summary: The United States and China plan to have talks about artificial intelligence in September to discuss how both countries will develop and use these systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This is government policy discussion — nothing that affects your business or life right now, but it shows leaders are trying to work together on AI rules.

stories.title: The data center and AI donors powering Donalds’ bid for Florida governor
stories.simple_summary: Someone running for Florida governor is getting financial support from companies that own computer servers and AI technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is political news — nothing you need to act on unless you vote in Florida.

stories.title: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
stories.simple_summary: A company called Gritt raised $32-34 million to build robots that will do the hardest and most dangerous jobs on construction sites, like building solar panel installations.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're in construction or own a construction business, this shows that robots are starting to do heavy physical work — you may see this technology at job sites soon.

stories.title: China weighs tighter export controls on AI models and chips
stories.simple_summary: China is considering stricter rules on selling AI technology and computer chips to other countries.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly for government and business leaders — it won't directly affect you unless you work in international tech trade.

stories.title: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic, an AI company, has paid $1.5 billion to settle a lawsuit about using copyrighted songs and books to train its AI. A judge approved this settlement.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mostly important to lawyers and investors — it suggests AI companies may have to pay creators for the work they use, but this one settlement doesn't solve the whole problem.

stories.title: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
stories.simple_summary: The person chosen to lead a government office focused on AI standards has already quit.
stories.why_it_matters: This is government news — nothing that affects your daily life, but it shows there's confusion about how the government should handle AI.

stories.title: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
stories.simple_summary: Sony Music is suing Udio, a company that makes AI music, claiming it copied more than 30,000 of Sony's songs — including famous ones by Elvis, Beyoncé, and Harry Styles.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows record companies are fighting to protect their artists from AI music makers copying their work — it's part of a bigger question about whether AI should be allowed to learn from existing music.
